title: Boat Rental in Dubrovnik: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Dubrovnik, with its ancient walls and shimmering Adriatic, is a dream destination for exploring the Elaphiti Islands by sea. Renting a boat from the Old Port starts from around €250 for a half-day motorboat, and you can compare options on BoatHire24 to find the perfect vessel for your group.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dubrovnik?

Prices range from €250 for a small motorboat half-day to €3,000 for a crewed luxury yacht per day in high season.

Shared tour tickets start at €50 per person for a three-hour sightseeing trip. A 40hp speedboat for up to five people costs about €250 for four hours. Catamarans like the Lagoon 42 can be chartered from €800 for a full day, while a fully crewed Azimut 55 flybridge may exceed €2,500. For a comprehensive overview of verified listings, visit yacht charter in Dubrovnik on the platform.

Where to set off in Dubrovnik

Most charters depart from the Old Town harbour, but you can also start from the modern ACI Marina Dubrovnik in Komolac, which has 430 berths and full facilities. Further south, the harbour at Cavtat offers a quieter alternative. The Elaphiti Islands of Šipan, Lopud and Koločep are all within 12 nautical miles, making them ideal for day trips. The sandy beach at Šunj on Lopud is a favourite anchorage. Just north, the town of Slano has a small marina and is a good base for exploring the mainland coast.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 m), Best for couples or small groups wanting to explore coves; carries up to 5 people; from €250 for a half-day.
  2. Sports boat (e.g. Axopar 37), Ideal for speed and day trips; seats up to 10; from €600 for a full day.
  3. Sailing yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 50), A classic choice for a relaxed cruise; sleeps 6-8; from €1,200 for 24 hours.
  4. Catamaran (Lagoon 42), Spacious and stable, perfect for families; capacity 10-12; from €800 for a day charter.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (Princess V58), For a premium experience with crew; accommodates 6 guests; from €2,000 per day. See the Princess V58 charter page for full details.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Dubrovnik?

For most motorboats up to 15 hp (or 30 hp for residents of certain countries) no licence is needed, but requirements vary by country of origin.

Tip: always confirm the licence policy with the operator when booking, as Croatian authorities require an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a national licence for larger engines. The rental company will advise exactly what you need.

High season runs from June to September, when the maestral wind provides reliable afternoon breezes for sailors. In July and August, temperatures reach 30°C and demand is highest.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50 (shared tour) to €3,000+ (crewed yacht) per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip: half-day (4 hrs) or full-day (8 hrs)
  • Guest capacity: 5, 12 depending on boat type
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Dubrovnik

Can I rent a boat in Dubrovnik without a licence?

Yes, you can rent small motorboats with engines up to 15 hp without a licence, provided you are over 18 and have basic boating experience.

What is the best month for a boat trip in Dubrovnik?

June and September offer warm weather, fewer crowds and lower prices than July and August, making them ideal for a relaxed charter.

How do I book a boat in Dubrovnik?

You can browse and book directly through online platforms like BoatHire24, which show real availability, prices and verified owner reviews.

What should I bring for a day boat rental?

Sunscreen, a hat, swimwear, water shoes and a light jacket for the evening breeze are essential, plus your passport or ID for check-in.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubrovnik and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
